# If there is no publication information, please cite Investigators, Title, and Online_Resource and date accessed.

#       Description: NOAA Landing Page

#       Description:

# Description/Documentation lines begin with #

# Parameter_Keywords: age control, oxygen isotopes, hydrogen isotopes
#--------------------
# Contribution_Date
#	Date: 2020-03-26
#--------------------
# File_Last_Modified_Date
#	Date: 2020-03-26
#--------------------
# Title
#	Study_Name: Scarisoara Ice Cave, Romania Holocene Stable Isotope Data 
#--------------------
# Investigators
#	Investigators: Persoiu, A.; Onac, B.P.; Wynn, J.G.; Blaauw, M.; Ionita, M.; Hansson, M.
#--------------------
# Description_Notes_and_Keywords
#	Description: Stable isotope (d18O, d2H, d-excess) data from Scarisoara ice cave, Carpathian Mountains, Romania, for the past 10,000 years.
#--------------------
# Publication 
#	Authors: Aurel Persoiu, Bogdan P. Onac, Jonathan G. Wynn, Maarten Blaauw, Monica Ionita, Margareta Hansson 
#	Published_Date_or_Year: 2017-04-26     
#	Published_Title: Holocene winter climate variability in Central and Eastern Europe 
#	Journal_Name: Scientific Reports
#	Volume: 7
#	Edition: 1196
#	Issue: 
#	Pages:
#	Report_Number: 
#	DOI: 10.1038/s41598-017-01397-w
#	Online_Resource: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-01397-w
#	Full_Citation: 
#	Abstract: Among abundant reconstructions of Holocene climate in Europe, only a handful has addressed winter conditions, and most of these are restricted in length and/or resolution. Here we present a record of late autumn through early winter air temperature and moisture source changes in East-Central Europe for the Holocene, based on stable isotopic analysis of an ice core recovered from a cave in the Romanian Carpathian Mountains. During the past 10,000 years, reconstructed temperature changes followed insolation, with a minimum in the early Holocene, followed by gradual and continuous increase towards the mid-to-late-Holocene peak (between 4-2 kcal BP), and finally by a decrease after 0.8 kcal BP towards a minimum during the Little Ice Age (AD 1300-1850). Reconstructed early Holocene atmospheric circulation patterns were similar to those characteristics of the negative phase of the North Atlantic Oscillation (NAO), while in the late Holocene they resembled those prevailing in the positive NAO phase. The transition between the two regimes occurred abruptly at around 4.7 kcal BP. Remarkably, the widespread cooling at 8.2 kcal BP is not seen very well as a temperature change, but as a shift in moisture source, suggesting weaker westerlies and increased Mediterranean cyclones penetrating northward at this time.
#------------------

# 	Elevation: 1165
#------------------
# Data_Collection   
#	Collection_Name: Scarisoara2017age
#	Earliest_Year: 10113
#	Most_Recent_Year: 88
#	Time_Unit: Cal. Year BP
#	Core_Length: 22.53
#	Notes: Results of age-depth modeling, Scarisoara Ice Cave, Romania
#------------------
# Chronology_Information
#	Chronology: 
# 	Results of radiocarbon analysis of organic matter from the ice block in Scarisoara Ice Cave, Romania				
# 			
# Sample name	Lab #	Original depth (cm)	14C age (year BP±1s)	Remark
# Surface	non 14C	24	90 ± 20 cal BP	based on ice level measurements, see the article
# SCL22	Poz-28925	72	355 ± 30 BP	TOC
# SCL23	Poz-00000	150	>0 BP	NO
# SCL24	Poz-28933	431	705 ± 30 BP	TOC
# N14	Poz-00000	476.5	>0 BP	NO
# SCL27	Poz-28928	497	630 ± 50 BP	TOC, 0.11mgC
# SCL25	Poz-28926	675	1140 ± 35 BP	TOC, 0.25mgC
# N3	Poz-28883	682.5	1110 ± 50 BP	TOC, 0.18mgC
# SCL26	Poz-28927	709.5	1220 ± 30 BP	TOC
# N15	Poz-00000	726	>0 BP	NO
# SCL11	Poz-28920	735	65 ± 30 BP	TOC
# N17	Poz-28916	880	1380 ± 40 BP	TOC, 0.15mgC
# N2	Poz-28882	968	1730 ± 50 BP	TOC, 0.2mgC
# SCL12	Poz-00000	974	>0 BP	NO
# N7	Poz-00000	991	>0 BP	NO

# Variables 
#
# Data variables follow are preceded by "##" in columns one and two.
# Data line variables format:  one per line, shortname-tab-variable components (what, material, error, units, seasonality, data type,detail, method, C or N for Character or Numeric data, free text) 
#
## depth	depth, , , centimeter, , ice cores, , ,N, cm below surface
## min	age, , , calendar year before present, , ice cores, , ,N,age minimum
## max	age, , , calendar year before present, , ice cores, , ,N,age maximum
## med	age, , , calendar year before present, , ice cores, , ,N,age median
## wmean	age, , , calendar year before present, , ice cores, , ,N,single best model used in this work
